framing_brief: Expat Saudi residents want to buy property remotely, verified via Nafath. The obstacle is trust + liquidity; diagnosis is that existing portals optimize for listings, not for verified transactions. Guiding policy: position as 'Nafath-verified property transactions', not 'property listings'. Coherent action: 3 pilot cities, SAMA-regulated escrow partner, Arabic/Urdu/Tagalog funnels.

lenses.strategic_kernel: Diagnosis: the portals are list-led; expats need transaction-verified trust. Guiding policy: Nafath-anchored transactions. Coherent action: Riyadh/Jeddah/Dammam pilots + SAMA-regulated escrow partner.
lenses.product_shape: User: Saudi-resident South Asian expat buying first property remotely. Outcome: 100 completed Nafath-verified transactions in 12 months. Non-goals: not a listing portal, not a brokerage, not financing.
lenses.build_class: Retrofit onto existing SaaS CRM (Salesforce REIQ) — build Nafath integration + escrow API. Buy the listings layer (partner with Bayut feed).
lenses.economic_shape: Transaction fee 0.5% of property value → avg SAR 7,500 per deal at SAR 1.5M median. CAC target < SAR 2,000. Break-even at 500 deals / year.
lenses.market_bet: Position as 'the only Nafath-verified property transaction platform in Saudi'. Fight for shelf space against Bayut (listings-led, no transaction trust) and manual brokers (high-touch but slow). Category: transaction-verified real estate.
